system.description: While any piece of cord can be used to strangle someone, a garrote has handles to make it easier to apply force. A garrote can only be used on someone who has been successfully grappled or surprised. If a successful grapple leads to the enemy suffering damage, they suffer 2d6 damage. Every consecutive round a garrote is applied with a successful grapple check, add an additional 1d6 damage, in addition to any suffocation damage the victim may take.
